agreement_notes: Pronoun assumption rule — 'Yeye ni' (He/she is) implies the subject's gender before identifying their role. In Swahili (pro-drop language), stating the subject pronoun is often unnecessary. WARN only — cannot auto-replace because 'Yeye ni' also appears in anaphoric reference (prior subject named), quoted speech, and neutral factual contexts. Only genuine bias when: (1) no named referent earlier in text AND (2) occupation is strongly gendered. Requires human review to confirm.

Pronoun assumption — 'Yeye anafanya' implies a gendered subject. Downgraded to warn: fires on neutral sentences like 'Yeye anafanya kazi kama yangu' (he does work like mine). Only bias when gender assumption is clear from context.
Gender-marked suffix (female)
Gender-marked suffix (male)

example_biased: Yeye ni mama wa nyumbani tu

agreement_notes: Factual marital reference — mke wa X is biographical/factual in most contexts. Flagging as warn only to avoid false positives on sentences like 'Mke wa Rais alihudhuria sherehe' or 'Winnie Mandela mke wa Nelson Mandela'. Replace-severity would fire on nearly all marital references regardless of whether they imply bias.
constraints: quote|biographical

agreement_notes: Factual marital reference — mume wa X fires on neutral biographical sentences (e.g. 'mume wa daktari alikuja' meaning 'the doctor's husband came'). Downgraded to warn like mke wa — auto-replacement would convert all husband-references regardless of bias context.
constraints: biographical|quote

agreement_notes: Generic possessive — WARN only when human occupation referent is present. zake agrees with ki-vi or other noun classes, not human gender. False positive risk is very high on neutral sentences.
constraints: Only flag when preceded within 40 chars by a human occupation noun (m-wa class). zake is a standard noun-class possessive concord — firing on inanimate nouns (nyumba, kadi, matokeo) is a false positive. Required human-referent pattern: \b(daktari|muuguzi|mwalimu|rubani|dereva|mhandisi|wakili|mbunge|waziri|rais|koch...

agreement_notes: Generic possessive — WARN only when human occupation referent is present. High false positive risk: mke wake, mume wake are factual family refs, not bias.
constraints: Only flag when preceded within 40 chars by a human occupation noun (m-wa class). wake agrees with m-wa class but fires on any m-wa noun including inanimate ones. Avoid: mwaka wake, mkoba wake, msururu wake, mto wake.

agreement_notes: Generic possessive — WARN only when human occupation referent is present. Extremely high false positive risk: yake is one of most common Swahili words.
constraints: Only flag when preceded within 40 chars by a human occupation noun. yake agrees with n, i-zi, u-i noun classes — virtually all inanimate. Firing on kadi yake, nyumba yake, kazi yake, shule yake is a false positive.
